Class MainView

java.lang.Object
View.MainView
public class MainView extends Object
The View component of the MVC pattern, responsible for all visual rendering and displaying game state information (lives, score, level) using JavaFX. It maps game coordinates to canvas pixels and handles sprite drawing. * @author Luis Carlos / Nathan Ng (assuming based on prior files)

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    MainView()
    Initializes the entire view structure, including the canvas, the drawing context, and the layout for the information bar.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clearCanvas()
    Clears the entire canvas drawing area before the next frame is drawn.
    javafx.scene.Scene
    getScene()
    Returns the JavaFX scene containing the game view.
    void
    renderBombs(ArrayList<Bomb> bombs)
    Renders all active Bomb objects, including the bomb sprite ('B') and the full blast radius when the bomb explodes ('!').
    void
    renderEnemies(ArrayList<Enemy> enemies)
    Renders all active generic Enemy objects.
    void
    renderHero(Hero hero)
    Renders the Hero at their current grid position.
    void
    renderMap(Map map)
    Renders the static and breakable map elements based on the content of the Map grid.
    void
    renderPowerUps(ArrayList<PowerUp> powerUps)
    Renders all active, uncollected PowerUp objects.
    void
    renderProjectiles(ArrayList<Projectile> projectiles)
    Renders active Projectile objects.
    void
    renderSnakes(ArrayList<Snake> snakes)
    Renders all active Snake enemy objects.
    void
    updateInfo(int lives, int score, int level)
    Updates the score, lives, and level information displayed in the top bar.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • MainView

      public MainView()
      Initializes the entire view structure, including the canvas, the drawing context, and the layout for the information bar.

  • Method Details

    • getScene

      public javafx.scene.Scene getScene()
      Returns the JavaFX scene containing the game view.
      Returns:
      The primary Scene object.

    • clearCanvas

      public void clearCanvas()
      Clears the entire canvas drawing area before the next frame is drawn.

    • renderMap

      public void renderMap(Map map)
      Renders the static and breakable map elements based on the content of the Map grid. Note: Dynamic entities ('H', 'E', 'S', 'B', '!', P, L) are typically rendered by separate methods layered on top.
      Parameters:
      map - The game map object containing the grid data.

    • renderHero

      public void renderHero(Hero hero)
      Renders the Hero at their current grid position.
      Parameters:
      hero - The hero object to render.

    • renderEnemies

      public void renderEnemies(ArrayList<Enemy> enemies)
      Renders all active generic Enemy objects.
      Parameters:
      enemies - The list of active generic enemies.

    • renderSnakes

      public void renderSnakes(ArrayList<Snake> snakes)
      Renders all active Snake enemy objects.
      Parameters:
      snakes - The list of active snake enemies.

    • renderBombs

      public void renderBombs(ArrayList<Bomb> bombs)
      Renders all active Bomb objects, including the bomb sprite ('B') and the full blast radius when the bomb explodes ('!').
      Parameters:
      bombs - The list of active bombs.

    • renderProjectiles

      public void renderProjectiles(ArrayList<Projectile> projectiles)
      Renders active Projectile objects.
      Parameters:
      projectiles - The list of active projectiles from the Controller.

    • renderPowerUps

      public void renderPowerUps(ArrayList<PowerUp> powerUps)
      Renders all active, uncollected PowerUp objects. It uses the instanceof check to draw the correct sprite for specific subtypes like ExtraLife.
      Parameters:
      powerUps - The list of active power-ups from the Controller.

    • updateInfo

      public void updateInfo(int lives, int score, int level)
      Updates the score, lives, and level information displayed in the top bar.
      Parameters:
      lives - The current number of hero lives.
      score - The hero's current score.
      level - The current level number.
